model_narrative: We plan investment and operations for a national fertilizer industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we decide which production processes to run and at what level, how much capacity to add to each productive unit and whether to expand or convert capacity at all, how much of each final product to ship from each plant to each demand region, how much to import or export, and how much raw material to buy or import. Capacity built in one period stays available in later periods, and expansion or conversion decisions are on-off choices. Each period also carries a set of cost-category totals that roll up into one figure. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost of the whole plan, summing every cost category over every period after applying each period's discount factor.

sets.name: j
sets.doc: demand regions of the country that consume fertilizer

sets.name: i
sets.doc: plant locations where production, expansion and shipment originate

sets.name: m
sets.doc: productive units inside the plants, each providing a capacity that processes draw on

sets.name: me
sets.doc: the subset of productive units that are candidates for capacity expansion

sets.name: p
sets.doc: production processes; running a process consumes some commodities and yields others

sets.name: g
sets.doc: upgrading or conversion activities that re-purpose existing capacity from one unit to another

sets.name: cq
sets.doc: plant nutrients, nitrogen and phosphate, that final-product demand is expressed in

sets.name: c
sets.doc: all commodities: raw materials, intermediates and final products

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: final products, the fertilizers actually delivered to demand regions

sets.name: ci
sets.doc: intermediate products produced and consumed inside the plant system

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: intermediates that may be shipped between plants

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: raw materials that can be imported

sets.name: cd
sets.doc: domestically purchased miscellaneous materials that enter process operating cost

sets.name: q
sets.doc: cost categories that the total discounted cost is composed of

sets.name: t
sets.doc: planning periods in chronological order; investments made in one period stay available in later periods

params.name: alpha
params.doc: nutrient content of a commodity, the tons of a given nutrient per ton of the commodity

params.name: eta
params.doc: the largest share of a region's nitrogen demand that is allowed to be met by urea, per period

params.name: db
params.doc: nutrient demand of each region per period, in thousand tons per year of nutrient

params.name: eb
params.doc: the maximum export allowed for any single product in a period; applied as the upper bound on each export variable

params.name: eh

params.name: muf
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for shipping a final product from a plant to a demand region

params.name: mufv
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for delivering an imported final product to a demand region

params.name: mue
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for moving an export from a plant to the port

params.name: mui
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for an interplant shipment from one plant to another

params.name: mur
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for an imported raw material to a plant

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficient of a process; positive means the process yields the commodity, negative means it consumes it, per unit of process level

params.name: b
params.doc: units of a productive unit's capacity used per unit of process level; nonzero entries say which unit a process loads

params.name: oc
params.doc: cost of domestic miscellaneous inputs per unit of process level

params.name: pv
params.doc: import price of a commodity, cif in 1975 us dollars per ton; a final product can be imported only where this is nonzero

params.name: pe
params.doc: export price of a final product, in 1975 us dollars per ton

params.name: pd
params.doc: domestic purchase price of a raw material at a plant

params.name: k
params.doc: initial capacity of a productive unit at a plant, in thousand tons per year

params.name: omega
params.doc: fixed-charge portion of investment for a unit, incurred whenever the unit is expanded, in million le per year

params.name: omegag
params.doc: fixed-charge for a capacity-conversion activity, incurred whenever it is undertaken

params.name: nu
params.doc: variable portion of investment per unit of capacity added, in million le per thousand tons per year

params.name: hb
params.doc: the largest capacity expansion permitted for a unit in one period

params.name: f
params.doc: capacity made available to a unit at a plant when a conversion activity is undertaken

params.name: delta
params.doc: discount factor applied to a period's costs when forming the total discounted cost

params.name: ts
params.doc: time-summation matrix; an entry is one when the first period is at or after the second, marking which earlier investment periods are still in force in a given period

params.name: oc/pmisc
params.doc: see oc

params.name: nul
params.doc: labor man-years required per unit of capacity added

params.name: omegal
params.doc: fixed labor man-years required whenever a unit is expanded

params.name: plab
params.doc: price of labor in le per man-year

params.name: sigma
params.doc: capital recovery factor turning investment outlays into an annual charge

vars.name: z
vars.doc: level at which a process is run at a plant in a period, in thousand tons per year; defined only where the process can run at that plant

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: domestic shipment of a final product from a plant to a demand region in a period

vars.name: xi
vars.doc: interplant shipment of an intermediate, produced at the first plant and consumed at the second, in a period

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: import of a final product delivered to a demand region in a period; defined only for products that can be imported

vars.name: vr
vars.doc: import of a raw material to a plant in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: export of a final product from a plant in a period; each is capped by the per-product export bound

vars.name: et
vars.doc: total exports in a period across all products; capped by the total-export bound

vars.name: u
vars.doc: domestic purchase of a raw material at a plant in a period

vars.name: h
vars.doc: amount of capacity added to a unit at a plant in a period

vars.name: up
vars.doc: one if a conversion activity is undertaken at a plant in a period, zero otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: one if a unit is expanded at a plant in a period, zero otherwise; this indicator gates the size of the expansion

vars.name: psi
vars.doc: total discounted cost over the whole horizon, in million le; this is what the objective minimizes

vars.name: psic
vars.doc: undiscounted cost of each cost category in each period, in million le per year

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: For every nutrient and every demand region in each period, the nutrient delivered to that region through final products must be enough to meet that region's demand for that nutrient. Count the nutrient carried by each final product, whether it was shipped in from the plants or imported, weighted by how much of that nutrient the product contains, and require the total to be at least the region's demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In every demand region and period there is a limit on how much of the region's nitrogen demand may be supplied as urea. Take the nitrogen carried by all the urea reaching the region, whether shipped from the plants or imported, and require it to stay within the allowed share of that region's nitrogen demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: At each plant, for every commodity in every period, the amount available must cover the amount used up. What is produced by the processes running there, plus what arrives by interplant shipment, plus what is bought domestically or imported, must be at least what is consumed by those processes, what is shipped out to other plants, what is sent as final product to demand regions, and what is exported. A process adds to availability for the commodities it yields and draws down availability for the commodities it consumes.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: For every productive unit at every plant in each period, the demand placed on that unit by the processes running there cannot exceed the capacity available. The available capacity is the unit's starting capacity plus everything added through expansion and conversion in this period and in all earlier periods that are still in effect, where each expansion contributes a fixed fraction of its built amount.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Whenever a unit is expanded at a plant in a period, the size of that expansion cannot exceed the unit's maximum allowed expansion, and no expansion may take place at all unless the decision to expand has been switched on.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Each conversion activity at a plant may be carried out at most once over the entire horizon, so across all periods the activity can be undertaken in at most one of them.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the total exports figure must equal the sum of the individual product exports leaving the plants that period.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the capital charge equals the recovered cost of all investment that is in force that period, gathered over this period and every earlier period still in effect. Each expansion carries a fixed charge for being undertaken plus a charge proportional to the capacity built, each conversion carries its own fixed charge, the whole amount is scaled by the capital recovery factor, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the domestic recurrent cost equals the total spent buying raw materials domestically, valuing each plant's purchase of each material at its local price, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the operating cost equals the cost of the miscellaneous domestic inputs drawn by the running processes plus the labor cost of expansion. The labor cost combines a fixed labor requirement for each expansion undertaken and a labor requirement proportional to the capacity built, accumulated over this period and earlier periods still in effect, priced at the labor rate, and the whole is expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the transport cost equals the total cost of all movements that period. This covers shipping final products from plants to demand regions, delivering imported final products to regions, moving exports from plants to the port, interplant shipments of intermediates between plants, and bringing imported raw materials to plants, each valued at its own per-ton transport rate, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psic['work-cap',
constraints.intent: In each period, the working-capital cost is a fixed small fraction of the sum of the domestic recurrent cost, the operating cost, and the imported-raw-material cost for that period.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-raw-material cost equals what is spent importing raw materials to the plants, valuing each plant's import of each raw material at that material's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-final-product cost equals what is spent importing final products to the demand regions, valuing each import at that product's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, export revenue is recorded as a negative cost. It equals what is earned selling final products abroad, valuing each plant's export of each product at that product's export price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the sum of all of its cost categories.

query: We're looking at a foreign-exchange squeeze in the back end of the plan. Suppose we hold final-product imports in the 1985-87 period to no more than 50 thousand tons and let domestic plants pick up the rest — what total cost does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_import = Constraint(expr=sum(model.vf[c, j, model.t.last()] for (c, j, t) in model.vf if t == model.t.last()) <= 50)

query: The CAN-335 lane out of Aswan into the Assiout district carries a heavy load across the early periods. There's a proposal on the table to hold the combined Aswan-to-Assiout CAN-335 tonnage, summed over both opening periods, to no more than 40 thousand tons and let the network reroute the rest. What total program cost does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_can335_assiout = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xf['can-335', 'aswan', 'assiout', t] for t in model.t) <= 40)

Count the nutrient carried by each final product, whether it was shipped in from the plants or imported, weighted by how much of that nutrient the product contains, and require the total to be at least the region's demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In every demand region and period there is a limit on how much of the region's nitrogen demand may be supplied as urea. Take the nitrogen carried by all the urea reaching the region, whether shipped from the plants or imported, and require it to stay within the allowed share of that region's nitrogen demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: At each plant, for every commodity in every period, the amount available must cover the amount used up. What is produced by the processes running there, plus what arrives by interplant shipment, plus what is bought domestically or imported, must be at least what is consumed by those processes, what is shipped out to other plants, what is sent as final product to demand regions, and what is exported. A process adds to availability for the commodities it yields and draws down availability for the commodities it consumes.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: For every productive unit at every plant in each period, the demand placed on that unit by the processes running there cannot exceed the capacity available. The available capacity is the unit's starting capacity plus everything added through expansion and conversion in this period and in all earlier periods that are still in effect, where each expansion contributes a fixed fraction of its built amount.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Whenever a unit is expanded at a plant in a period, the size of that expansion cannot exceed the unit's maximum allowed expansion, and no expansion may take place at all unless the decision to expand has been switched on.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Each conversion activity at a plant may be carried out at most once over the entire horizon, so across all periods the activity can be undertaken in at most one of them.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the total exports figure must equal the sum of the individual product exports leaving the plants that period.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the capital charge equals the recovered cost of all investment that is in force that period, gathered over this period and every earlier period still in effect. Each expansion carries a fixed charge for being undertaken plus a charge proportional to the capacity built, each conversion carries its own fixed charge, the whole amount is scaled by the capital recovery factor, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the domestic recurrent cost equals the total spent buying raw materials domestically, valuing each plant's purchase of each material at its local price, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the operating cost equals the cost of the miscellaneous domestic inputs drawn by the running processes plus the labor cost of expansion. The labor cost combines a fixed labor requirement for each expansion undertaken and a labor requirement proportional to the capacity built, accumulated over this period and earlier periods still in effect, priced at the labor rate, and the whole is expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the transport cost equals the total cost of all movements that period. This covers shipping final products from plants to demand regions, delivering imported final products to regions, moving exports from plants to the port, interplant shipments of intermediates between plants, and bringing imported raw materials to plants, each valued at its own per-ton transport rate, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psic['work-cap',
constraints.intent: In each period, the working-capital cost is a fixed small fraction of the sum of the domestic recurrent cost, the operating cost, and the imported-raw-material cost for that period.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-raw-material cost equals what is spent importing raw materials to the plants, valuing each plant's import of each raw material at that material's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-final-product cost equals what is spent importing final products to the demand regions, valuing each import at that product's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, export revenue is recorded as a negative cost. It equals what is earned selling final products abroad, valuing each plant's export of each product at that product's export price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the sum of all of its cost categories.

query: Logistics is pitching a two-part rationalization for the Assiout corridor: hold the combined Aswan-to-Assiout CAN-335 tonnage over the two opening periods to no more than 55 thousand tons, and at the same time keep the combined Talkha-to-Assiout urea tonnage over those same periods under 80 thousand tons, so the district draws more on its own ssp-155 output. Apply both together and walk me through the program cost the optimizer lands on.
expected_pyomo:
model.cap_can335_lane = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xf['can-335', 'aswan', 'assiout', t] for t in model.t) <= 55)
model.cap_urea_assiout = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xf['urea', 'talkha', 'assiout', t] for t in model.t) <= 80)

model_narrative: We plan investment and operations for a national fertilizer industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we decide which production processes to run and at what level, how much capacity to add to each productive unit and whether to expand or convert capacity at all, how much of each final product to ship from each plant to each demand region, how much to import or export, and how much raw material to buy or import. Capacity built in one period stays available in later periods, and expansion or conversion decisions are on-off choices. Each period also carries a set of cost-category totals that roll up into one figure. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost of the whole plan, summing every cost category over every period after applying each period's discount factor.

sets.name: m

sets.name: me
sets.doc: the subset of productive units that are candidates for capacity expansion

sets.name: p
sets.doc: production processes; running a process consumes some commodities and yields others

sets.name: g
sets.doc: upgrading or conversion activities that re-purpose existing capacity from one unit to another

sets.doc: intermediate products produced and consumed inside the plant system

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: intermediates that may be shipped between plants

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: raw materials that can be imported

sets.name: cd
sets.doc: domestically purchased miscellaneous materials that enter process operating cost

sets.name: q
sets.doc: cost categories that the total discounted cost is composed of

params.doc: nutrient content of a commodity, the tons of a given nutrient per ton of the commodity

params.name: eta
params.doc: the largest share of a region's nitrogen demand that is allowed to be met by urea, per period

params.name: db
params.doc: nutrient demand of each region per period, in thousand tons per year of nutrient

params.name: eb
params.doc: the maximum export allowed for any single product in a period; applied as the upper bound on each export variable

params.name: eh
params.doc: the maximum total export across all products in a period; applied as the upper bound on the total-export variable

params.name: muf
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for shipping a final product from a plant to a demand region

params.name: mufv
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for delivering an imported final product to a demand region

params.name: mue
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for moving an export from a plant to the port

params.name: mui
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for an interplant shipment from one plant to another

params.name: mur
params.doc: transport cost in le per ton for an imported raw material to a plant

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficient of a process; positive means the process yields the commodity, negative means it consumes it, per unit of process level

params.name: b
params.doc: units of a productive unit's capacity used per unit of process level; nonzero entries say which unit a process loads

params.name: oc
params.doc: cost of domestic miscellaneous inputs per unit of process level

params.name: pv
params.doc: import price of a commodity, cif in 1975 us dollars per ton; a final product can be imported only where this is nonzero

params.name: pe
params.doc: export price of a final product, in 1975 us dollars per ton

params.name: pd
params.doc: domestic purchase price of a raw material at a plant

params.name: k
params.doc: initial capacity of a productive unit at a plant, in thousand tons per year

params.name: omega
params.doc: fixed-charge portion of investment for a unit, incurred whenever the unit is expanded, in million le per year

params.name: omegag
params.doc: fixed-charge for a capacity-conversion activity, incurred whenever it is undertaken

params.name: nu
params.doc: variable portion of investment per unit of capacity added, in million le per thousand tons per year

params.name: hb
params.doc: the largest capacity expansion permitted for a unit in one period

params.name: f
params.doc: capacity made available to a unit at a plant when a conversion activity is undertaken

params.name: delta
params.doc: discount factor applied to a period's costs when forming the total discounted cost

params.name: ts
params.doc: time-summation matrix; an entry is one when the first period is at or after the second, marking which earlier investment periods are still in force in a given period

params.name: oc/pmisc
params.doc: see oc

params.name: nul
params.doc: labor man-years required per unit of capacity added

params.name: omegal
params.doc: fixed labor man-years required whenever a unit is expanded

params.name: plab
params.doc: price of labor in le per man-year

params.name: sigma
params.doc: capital recovery factor turning investment outlays into an annual charge

vars.name: z
vars.doc: level at which a process is run at a plant in a period, in thousand tons per year; defined only where the process can run at that plant

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: domestic shipment of a final product from a plant to a demand region in a period

vars.name: xi
vars.doc: interplant shipment of an intermediate, produced at the first plant and consumed at the second, in a period

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: import of a final product delivered to a demand region in a period; defined only for products that can be imported

vars.name: vr
vars.doc: import of a raw material to a plant in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: export of a final product from a plant in a period; each is capped by the per-product export bound

vars.name: et
vars.doc: total exports in a period across all products; capped by the total-export bound

vars.name: u
vars.doc: domestic purchase of a raw material at a plant in a period

vars.name: h
vars.doc: amount of capacity added to a unit at a plant in a period

vars.name: up
vars.doc: one if a conversion activity is undertaken at a plant in a period, zero otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: one if a unit is expanded at a plant in a period, zero otherwise; this indicator gates the size of the expansion

vars.name: psi
vars.doc: total discounted cost over the whole horizon, in million le; this is what the objective minimizes

vars.name: psic
vars.doc: undiscounted cost of each cost category in each period, in million le per year

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: For every nutrient and every demand region in each period, the nutrient delivered to that region through final products must be enough to meet that region's demand for that nutrient. Count the nutrient carried by each final product, whether it was shipped in from the plants or imported, weighted by how much of that nutrient the product contains, and require the total to be at least the region's demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In every demand region and period there is a limit on how much of the region's nitrogen demand may be supplied as urea. Take the nitrogen carried by all the urea reaching the region, whether shipped from the plants or imported, and require it to stay within the allowed share of that region's nitrogen demand.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: At each plant, for every commodity in every period, the amount available must cover the amount used up. What is produced by the processes running there, plus what arrives by interplant shipment, plus what is bought domestically or imported, must be at least what is consumed by those processes, what is shipped out to other plants, what is sent as final product to demand regions, and what is exported. A process adds to availability for the commodities it yields and draws down availability for the commodities it consumes.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: For every productive unit at every plant in each period, the demand placed on that unit by the processes running there cannot exceed the capacity available. The available capacity is the unit's starting capacity plus everything added through expansion and conversion in this period and in all earlier periods that are still in effect, where each expansion contributes a fixed fraction of its built amount.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Whenever a unit is expanded at a plant in a period, the size of that expansion cannot exceed the unit's maximum allowed expansion, and no expansion may take place at all unless the decision to expand has been switched on.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: Each conversion activity at a plant may be carried out at most once over the entire horizon, so across all periods the activity can be undertaken in at most one of them.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the total exports figure must equal the sum of the individual product exports leaving the plants that period.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the capital charge equals the recovered cost of all investment that is in force that period, gathered over this period and every earlier period still in effect. Each expansion carries a fixed charge for being undertaken plus a charge proportional to the capacity built, each conversion carries its own fixed charge, the whole amount is scaled by the capital recovery factor, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the domestic recurrent cost equals the total spent buying raw materials domestically, valuing each plant's purchase of each material at its local price, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the operating cost equals the cost of the miscellaneous domestic inputs drawn by the running processes plus the labor cost of expansion. The labor cost combines a fixed labor requirement for each expansion undertaken and a labor requirement proportional to the capacity built, accumulated over this period and earlier periods still in effect, priced at the labor rate, and the whole is expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the transport cost equals the total cost of all movements that period. This covers shipping final products from plants to demand regions, delivering imported final products to regions, moving exports from plants to the port, interplant shipments of intermediates between plants, and bringing imported raw materials to plants, each valued at its own per-ton transport rate, expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psic['work-cap',
constraints.intent: In each period, the working-capital cost is a fixed small fraction of the sum of the domestic recurrent cost, the operating cost, and the imported-raw-material cost for that period.

constraints.name: z}
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-raw-material cost equals what is spent importing raw materials to the plants, valuing each plant's import of each raw material at that material's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, the imported-final-product cost equals what is spent importing final products to the demand regions, valuing each import at that product's import price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: z[p,
constraints.intent: In each period, export revenue is recorded as a negative cost. It equals what is earned selling final products abroad, valuing each plant's export of each product at that product's export price, converted at the official exchange rate, and expressed in million le.

constraints.name: psi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the sum of all of its cost categories.

query: In the opening period the Assiout district leans heavily on long-haul CAN-335 trucked down from Aswan instead of leaning on the ssp-155 made right there in Assiout. Shouldn't the inbound CAN-335 from Aswan stay capped at no more than 30 percent of the combined inbound CAN-335-plus-local-ssp-155 tonnage that district takes in that period? What's keeping the model from leaning more on the local product?

model_narrative: We plan investment and operations for a national fertilizer industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we decide which production processes to run and at what level, how much capacity to add to each productive unit and whether to expand or convert capacity at all, how much of each final product to ship from each plant to each demand region, how much to import or export, and how much raw material to buy or import. Capacity built in one period stays available in later periods, and expansion or conversion decisions are on-off choices. Each period also carries a set of cost-category totals that roll up into one figure. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost of the whole plan, summing every cost category over every period after applying each period's discount factor.

sets.doc: the subset of productive units that are candidates for capacity expansion

sets.doc: production processes; running a process consumes some commodities and yields others

sets.name: g
sets.doc: upgrading or conversion activities that re-purpose existing capacity from one unit to another

sets.name: ci

sets.name: cs

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: raw materials that can be imported

sets.name: cd
sets.doc: domestically purchased miscellaneous materials that enter process operating cost

sets.name: q
sets.doc: cost categories that the total discounted cost is composed of

query: The Assiout phosphate plant sits right next to the Assiout demand district, yet the plan keeps its local ssp-155 deliveries into that district fairly modest in places. Shouldn't every period see at least 60 thousand tons of locally made ssp-155 going into the Assiout district to lean on that nearby supply? What's keeping the model from committing that much local product in each period?
